mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with funds rate changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.4pp monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions upward and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.05 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and rising input costs, which compress profit margins and force negative S&P 500 adjustments over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during tightening periods generate positive 20-day forward returns in over 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 3.5% triggers immediate expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and forces rapid equity deleveraging, producing negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a cumulative alpha of less than 1% over a 4-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly declines in real gross domestic product indicate broad economic contraction, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and triggers systematic portfolio rebalancing away from equities, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Negative GDP growth quarters are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Expansion in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals successful capacity utilization and demand fulfillment, which boosts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth during recovery phases fails to predict positive 5-day returns, with a hit rate below 45% over a 3-year walk-forward evaluation.
